articles.title: HOUSE PRICE ANALYSIS BY COUNTRY: Scotland remains most affordable
articles.summary: In her latest review of property prices by UK region, Kate Faulkner gives a more detailed analysis of the market in Scotland where it is currently cheapest. The post HOUSE PRICE ANALYSIS BY COUNTRY: Scotland remains most affordable appeared first on The Negotiator.

articles.title: Properties near marathon routes listed across England
articles.summary: Five residential properties located along marathon and half-marathon routes across England have been listed for sale, with prices ranging from £350,000 to £1.6 million. The listings span from Brighton to Yorkshire, including properties in London's Dalston and Woolwich areas. The post Properties near marathon routes listed across England appeared first on PropertyWire.

articles.title: Landlord property sales decline 45% as rental exodus slows
articles.summary: The proportion of landlords selling former rental properties has fallen by almost half over the past year, according to new data from TwentyCi. The share of homes coming to market that were previously rented dropped from 22.5% in Q1 2025 to 12.4% in Q1 2026, representing a year-on-year reduction of 45%. The post Landlord property sales decline 45% as rental exodus slows appeared first on PropertyWire.

articles.title: Investor group challenges leasehold reforms over £30bn losses
articles.summary: A coalition of investors and freeholders has launched a campaign to challenge leasehold reforms, claiming losses could exceed £30 billion. The group, Justice for Property Rights, is seeking legal advice on bringing a collective action before the European Court of Human Rights. The post Investor group challenges leasehold reforms over £30bn losses appeared first on PropertyWire.

articles.title: Property transactions take over 17 weeks to exchange
articles.summary: Property transactions in the UK are taking significantly longer to complete, with 43% of housing sales now requiring more than 17 weeks to reach exchange. The trade body described the figure as the highest level recorded, highlighting ongoing inefficiencies in the transaction process. The post Property transactions take over 17 weeks to exchange appeared first on PropertyWire.

articles.title: UK property transactions down 7.6% year-on-year in 2026
articles.summary: UK property sales have declined 7.6% year-to-date compared to 2025, with 342,000 homes sold subject to contract by mid-April 2026. The figures show transactions remain below last year's levels but continue to outperform 2024 by 5.6% and sit 12.9% above pre-Covid norms. The post UK property transactions down 7.6% year-on-year in 2026 appeared first on PropertyWire.

articles.title: Government restates opposition to rent controls in clear split with Greens
articles.summary: Greens and Labour clash over rent policy, with Housing Minister Matthew Pennycook claiming caps would make life more difficult for renters. The post Government restates opposition to rent controls in clear split with Greens appeared first on The Negotiator.

articles.title: Letting agents plan to raise fees ahead of Renters’ Rights Act
articles.summary: Many agents will increase the fees they charge landlords, but risk losing them as clients, according to lettings tech firm Goodlord. The post Letting agents plan to raise fees ahead of Renters’ Rights Act appeared first on The Negotiator.

articles.title: Rental rules can’t be enforced with £60m, claims leading lawyer
articles.summary: David Smith, Property Litigation Partner at Spector Constant & Williams, warns funding will not fix chronic shortages of experienced enforcement officers. The post Rental rules can’t be enforced with £60m, claims leading lawyer appeared first on The Negotiator.

articles.title: Landlords ‘set to pull more than 200,000 rental properties this year’
articles.summary: An estimated 220,000 homes will be withdrawn, with 65,000 due to the Renters' Rights Act, according to new research. The post Landlords ‘set to pull more than 200,000 rental properties this year’ appeared first on The Negotiator.

articles.title: Investors seek share of ‘£30bn’ leasehold reform losses
articles.summary: The coalition of investors, retirees and freeholders, is calling on the Government not go ahead with the existing leasehold reforms. The post Investors seek share of ‘£30bn’ leasehold reform losses appeared first on The Negotiator.

articles.title: SNP ‘first refusal’ housing proposal comes under fire
articles.summary: A proposal by Scottish First Minister, SNP leader John Swinney, to give private renters ‘first refusal’ to buy their homes, has come under fire from a property policy expert. The post SNP ‘first refusal’ housing proposal comes under fire appeared first on Property Investor Today.

articles.title: England faces historic housing shortfall – households set to surge 17% by 2040
articles.summary: The number of households in England is projected to rise by 17% to 27.6 million by 2040 causing significant demographic change. The post England faces historic housing shortfall – households set to surge 17% by 2040 appeared first on Property Investor Today.

articles.title: Northern Ireland’s top emerging investment hotspots
articles.summary: Northern Ireland’s emerging investment hotspots are delivering compelling opportunities for investors in 2026, with new research from Belfast-based estate agency John Minnis revealing a shift in where landlords are finding the strongest returns. The post Northern Ireland’s top emerging investment hotspots appeared first on Property Investor Today.

articles.title: Fleet Mortgages launches new BTL 2-year tracker
articles.summary: Fleet Mortgages, the buy-to-let specialist lender, has today (10th April 2026) announced the launch of three new 75% LTV two-year tracker products available within its three buy-to-let lending ranges: Standard, Limited Company and HMO/Multi-Unit Freehold Block (MUFB). The post Fleet Mortgages launches new BTL 2-year tracker appeared first on Property Investor Today.

articles.title: Landlords turn to HMOs to boost returns
articles.summary: A major shift is underway in the UK rental market, with an increasing number of landlords moving to Houses in Multiple Occupations (HMOs). The post Landlords turn to HMOs to boost returns appeared first on Property Investor Today.

articles.title: Yields up but war dents confidence, says Fleet
articles.summary: Rental yields across England and Wales increased across every region annually in Q1 2026, offering a positive start to the year for landlords, according to Fleet Mortgages’ latest Quarterly Rental Barometer. The post Yields up but war dents confidence, says Fleet appeared first on Property Investor Today.

articles.title: New data shows Scotland’s appeal to Gulf buyers
articles.summary: Scotland is becoming one of the most sought-after UK property markets for Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) buyers, driven by strong price growth, resilient demand and growing international appeal, according to new data released by Sharia’a‑compliant digital The post New data shows Scotland’s appeal to Gulf buyers appeared first on Property Investor Today.
